title: feat(observability): route haiku-gate-passed through structured event sink
body:
## From T5 overnight cleanup

`src/pipeline/runner.ts:173` currently logs `reviewer:haiku-gate-passed task=… round=… gate=…` via `console.warn`. That keeps lint happy (the no-console rule allows `warn`/`error`) but it is semantically an observability event, not a warning, and it does not flow through the event log that other reviewer-step events use.

## Acceptance criteria

- Emit a structured `reviewer.haiku_gate_passed` event via the existing observability layer (whatever pipeline events use today).
- Remove the `console.warn` from `runner.ts`.
- Tests assert on the event log, not on the console spy.

from:T5

title: fix(mcp,classifier): wire MCP submitSprint to classifier SprintMode via label
body:
## From T5 overnight review

The MCP `submitSprint` tool (`src/mcp/tools/submit-sprint.ts:75-79`) writes the mode into the issue body as:

```
<!-- source: mcp -->
<!-- mode: overnight -->

<brief>
```

T4's done note describes this as the "documented contract" for the classifier to pick up. **But the classifier does not read it.** `detectExplicitMode` in `src/classifier/modes.ts:92` uses regex `/^\s*mode\s*:\s*([a-z]+)\s*$/im` — that does not match `<!-- mode: foo -->` because of the `<!--` prefix.

## Options

1. Update the classifier regex to also match HTML-commented directives: `/^\s*(?:<!--\s*)?mode\s*:\s*([a-z]+)\s*(?:-->)?\s*$/im`.
2. Update MCP `renderBody()` to emit a plain `mode: foo` line instead of an HTML comment.
3. Or: emit a GitHub label (`mode:foo`) when MCP submits the issue — the classifier already detects `mode:*` labels (`detectExplicitMode` lines 83–90).

Option (3) is cleanest because labels are first-class GitHub citizens (filterable, visible in the UI) and the classifier already prefers them. Recommend.

## Acceptance criteria

- A sprint submitted via `submitSprint({ mode: 'ralph', ... })` produces a routing decision with `mode === 'ralph'`.
- Tests cover all three signal sources (MCP body, plain `mode:` header, `mode:*` label).
- Pairs with the followup that swaps the literal enum (link the two issues).

from:T5

title: fix(mcp,classifier): wire MCP submitSprint to classifier SprintMode via label
body:
## From T5 overnight review

`src/mcp/tools/submit-sprint.ts:13` currently uses:

```ts
mode: z.enum(['normal', 'overnight']).optional()
```

These were the values of the OLD `SprintMode` union before T3's PR #104 renamed it to `SprintOperatingMode` and made the new `SprintMode` the classifier per-task modes (`standard | ralph | ulw | tdd | deslop`).

T4's own done report flagged this: *"swap the local z.enum(['normal','overnight']) literal in src/mcp/tools/submit-sprint.ts for the real SprintMode import once T3 has landed."*

## Acceptance criteria
- The MCP `submitSprint` `mode` field uses the classifier `SprintMode` values, NOT the operating-mode values.
- The body header emitted in `renderBody()` is in a form the classifier's `detectExplicitMode` will actually parse (see followup #B below — currently the HTML comment `<!-- mode: x -->` is NOT picked up by the regex `/^\s*mode\s*:\s*([a-z]+)\s*$/im`).
- MCP tests updated.
- Typecheck + lint + test pass.

from:T5

title: chore: brief library + dead-code audit + post-merge lint cleanup
body:
## Context

Issues #75 and #76 are stuck with `auto:failed` labels from the crash loop fixed in PR #97. The queue now correctly skips `auto:failed` issues (terminal state), so they won't be retried automatically.

## Why NOT yet

The root cause of the original failure is **undiagnosed**: the pipeline editor returned `ok=true` with an empty diff. This is a silent failure — the worker subprocess's stdio was set to `'ignore'` (PR #95), leaving no logs. Stripping `auto:failed` before fixing the underlying bug means #75 and #76 will re-run, hit the same silent failure, and re-acquire the label.

## Unblock order

1. PR #97 merged ✅ — crash loop broken
2. Worker stdio routing shipped (follow-up to PR #97) — `worker-${issueNumber}.log` now captures output
3. Trigger a new sprint on any issue to produce a log → inspect `.omc/logs/worker-<n>.log` → diagnose the editor-produces-no-diff root cause
4. Fix the editor bug
5. **Then** strip `auto:failed` from #75 and #76 so the fleet retries them

## How to strip the labels (when ready)

```bash
gh issue edit 75 --remove-label "auto:failed" --repo weautomatehq1/IFleet
gh issue edit 76 --remove-label "auto:failed" --repo weautomatehq1/IFleet
```

## Labels
`auto:failed` issues: #75, #76

diff --git a/docs/T5-audit.md b/docs/T5-audit.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..dd2903b
--- /dev/null
+++ b/docs/T5-audit.md
@@ -0,0 +1,51 @@
+# T5 Dead-Code & Lint Audit
+
+Baseline before T1–T4 merge. Captured 2026-05-18 at start of Phase 0 from `chore/T5-cleanup-review` worktree, ref `925ec62` (main).
+
+## Baseline health
+
+| Check | Result |
+|---|---|
+| `pnpm typecheck` | clean, 0 errors |
+| `pnpm test` (node:test) | 251 pass / 0 fail / 0 skipped |
+| `pnpm test` (vitest) | 223 pass / 0 fail |
+| `pnpm lint` | 0 errors, 5 warnings |
+
+## Actionable lint warnings (5)
+
+```
+src/discord/client.ts:28:35       no-console  (console statement)
+src/discord/index.ts:50:5         no-console  (console statement)
+src/orchestrator/index.ts:310:7   no-console  (unused eslint-disable directive)
+src/pipeline/architect.ts:74:7    no-console  (unused eslint-disable directive)
+src/pipeline/runner.ts:163:9      no-console  (console statement)
+```
+
+Two of these (`orchestrator/index.ts:310`, `pipeline/architect.ts:74`) are stale `// eslint-disable-next-line no-console` directives — the lines they covered no longer have console calls. Safe to delete.
+
+Three are real `console.log` / `console.info` calls that the lint rule wants converted to `console.warn`/`console.error` or routed through the event log. Plan: in Phase 3, convert them to `console.warn` where appropriate (boot-time discord lifecycle messages are warn-level by convention) or remove if they duplicate the event log.
+
+## ts-prune output
+
+311 lines total, 169 after filtering `(used in module)`. **Most entries are false positives** — the IFleet root `tsconfig.json` doesn't include `scripts/` in its main program, so cross-file imports from `scripts/run-smoke.ts` etc. are invisible to ts-prune. Verified by grep:
+
+- `scripts/dispatcher-lock.ts:88 acquireDispatchLock` → imported by `scripts/run-smoke.ts:45` and 8x in tests
+- `src/workers/spawn-runner.ts:39 runStreaming` → imported by `claude.ts`, `codex.ts`, tests
+- `src/workers/types.ts:77 categorizeRateLimitError` → imported by `claude.ts`, `codex.ts`
+
+The vast majority of remaining entries are barrel re-exports from `src/*/index.ts` files. These are deliberate public API surface and **must not be deleted** — they let downstream packages import from a single path.
+
+Candidates that warrant a second look in Phase 3 (after T1–T4 merge — types may shift):
+
+| Path | Symbol | Risk |
+|---|---|---|
+| `src/contracts/task.ts:39,43` | `isDiscordSource`, `isGitHubSource` | Type guards. Likely intended for consumers; check after T1 lands `Task.mode` changes. |
+| `src/discord/slash-commands.ts:4` | `SlashCommandName` | Type alias for the union. Keep — public typing. |
+| `src/queue/control-plane.ts:328` | `signLegacyPayload` | Legacy HMAC. If no caller post-merge, delete. |
+
+## Recommendation for Phase 3
+
+1. Delete the two unused `eslint-disable-next-line` directives.
+2. Convert remaining 3 `no-console` warnings → `console.warn` (boot/lifecycle messages) or route through event log.
+3. Skip ts-prune-driven export deletion this sprint — too noisy without a proper tsconfig include for `scripts/`. File a followup to add `scripts/` to tsconfig include or `ts-prune --project tsconfig.json --error` integration.
+4. `pnpm lint --fix` after each change set.
diff --git a/docs/briefs/.gitkeep b/docs/briefs/.gitkeep
deleted file mode 100644
index e69de29..0000000
diff --git a/docs/briefs/README.md b/docs/briefs/README.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..cdf224f
--- /dev/null
+++ b/docs/briefs/README.md
@@ -0,0 +1,33 @@
+# Brief library
+
+Real-world example briefs derived from shipped IFleet sprints. Each file is one self-contained brief in the format the classifier and architect consume.
+
+## Frontmatter shape
+
+```yaml
+---
+id: <kebab-case slug, unique>
+title: <one-line conventional commit-style title>
+mode: ralph | ulw | tdd | deslop | default
+tags: [feature | bugfix | refactor | docs | test | chore | security, ...]
+source: <originating GitHub issue number, optional>
+---
+```
+
+After frontmatter the body is plain markdown: problem, acceptance criteria, and (when useful) out-of-scope.
+
+## Modes
+
+| Mode | When to pick |
+|---|---|
+| `ralph` | bug / broken / persistence loop until green |
+| `ulw` | multi-file refactor or feature touching 4+ files |
+| `tdd` | tests-first changes, behaviour-driven |
+| `deslop` | clean up dead code, comments, drift |
+| `default` | normal single-area changes |
+
+## How to use these
+
+- Treat them as templates — copy the structure, replace the content.
+- The classifier and learnings system read this directory at architect phase to build few-shot examples.
+- One file per brief, kebab-case names, ≤ 60 lines each.
diff --git a/docs/briefs/chore-remove-stale-tx-todos.md b/docs/briefs/chore-remove-stale-tx-todos.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..2eecf12
--- /dev/null
+++ b/docs/briefs/chore-remove-stale-tx-todos.md
@@ -0,0 +1,25 @@
+---
+id: chore-remove-stale-tx-todos
+title: "chore: remove stale \"until Tx lands\" TODO comments"
+mode: deslop
+tags: [chore, refactor]
+source: 6
+---
+
+## Problem
+
+After a multi-PR merge, several TODO comments referencing in-flight terminals are stale and create noise during review:
+
+- `src/orchestrator/sprint.ts:22` references "until T3 lands" — T3 has landed.
+- `src/orchestrator/types.ts:81` references "until T2 lands" — T2 has landed.
+
+## Acceptance criteria
+
+- The stale comment lines are removed (comments only, no code changes).
+- The imports immediately below them still resolve cleanly.
+- `pnpm test` continues to pass with the same test count.
+
+## Out of scope
+
+- Any code restructuring beyond comment deletion.
+- Renaming or moving the affected types.
diff --git a/docs/briefs/docs-adapter-self-registration-comment.md b/docs/briefs/docs-adapter-self-registration-comment.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..bbb4414
--- /dev/null
+++ b/docs/briefs/docs-adapter-self-registration-comment.md
@@ -0,0 +1,33 @@
+---
+id: docs-adapter-self-registration-comment
+title: "docs(adapters): update index.ts comment to explain pipeline-registry self-registration"
+mode: default
+tags: [docs, adapters]
+source: 66
+---
+
+## Problem
+
+The comment at the top of `src/workers/adapters/index.ts` says:
+
+> Import every adapter module so each one's `registerAdapter(...)` runs at load time. Adding a new backend = drop a file in this folder + add it here.
+
+This is now misleading. It only mentions `import './claude-cli.ts'` and implies every adapter needs an explicit side-effect import. But `pipeline-registry.ts` uses a different mechanism: it self-registers at the bottom of the module and activates via its re-export (no separate `import './pipeline-registry.ts'` line needed).
+
+## Expected behaviour
+
+The comment should accurately describe **both** registration patterns:
+
+1. **Orchestrator-level adapters** (e.g. `claude-cli.ts`) — explicit `import './module.ts'` side-effect triggers `registerAdapter(...)`.
+2. **Pipeline-level adapters** (e.g. `pipeline-registry.ts`) — self-registers at module bottom; the re-export `export { ... } from './pipeline-registry.ts'` is sufficient to trigger it.
+
+## Acceptance criteria
+
+- The comment block at the top of `src/workers/adapters/index.ts` (lines 1–3) is updated to explain both patterns.
+- `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` passes.
+- No other files changed.
+
+## Out of scope
+
+- Changing the registration mechanism itself.
+- Renaming any exports.
diff --git a/docs/briefs/feat-classifier-brief-to-routing.md b/docs/briefs/feat-classifier-brief-to-routing.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..6a6cdd4
--- /dev/null
+++ b/docs/briefs/feat-classifier-brief-to-routing.md
@@ -0,0 +1,31 @@
+---
+id: feat-classifier-brief-to-routing
+title: "feat(classifier): build brief → routing decision module"
+mode: ulw
+tags: [feature, classifier]
+source: 20
+---
+
+## Context
+
+`config/routing.json` defines keyword and file-glob rules for picking provider/model per task. Nothing reads it yet — routing is hardcoded in `scripts/run-smoke.ts`.
+
+## Goal
+
+Build `src/classifier/index.ts` that exports:
+
+```ts
+export function classifyTask(task: { title: string; body: string; labels: string[] }): RoutingDecision
+```
+
+- Load `config/routing.json` at startup
+- Match task title + body against `rules[].match.keywords` (case-insensitive substring)
+- Fall through to `pipeline` defaults when no rule matches
+- Return a `RoutingDecision` shaped like the existing type in `src/pipeline/types.ts`
+
+## Acceptance criteria
+
+- `classifyTask` returns correct provider/model for keyword matches
+- Falls back to `pipeline.architect` / `pipeline.editor` defaults when no rule matches
+- Unit tests cover: keyword hit, no match (default), multi-rule priority (first match wins)
+- Typecheck + lint + test pass
diff --git a/docs/briefs/feat-sprint-completed-duration.md b/docs/briefs/feat-sprint-completed-duration.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..562e463
--- /dev/null
+++ b/docs/briefs/feat-sprint-completed-duration.md
@@ -0,0 +1,36 @@
+---
+id: feat-sprint-completed-duration
+title: "feat(orchestrator): include durationMs in sprint.completed event payload"
+mode: tdd
+tags: [feature, orchestrator, observability]
+source: 29
+---
+
+## Problem
+
+When `SprintManager` emits `sprint.completed`, the payload has `from`/`to` but no timing info. Observability consumers must correlate separate events to compute sprint duration.
+
+## Spec — exactly what to change
+
+**`src/orchestrator/sprint.ts`** — in `transition()`, the `sprint.completed` branch currently emits:
+
+```ts
+{ from: current.state.kind, to: next.kind }
+```
+
+When `next.kind === 'completed'`, compute duration and add it:
+
+```ts
+const durationMs = current.state.kind === 'running' ? now - current.state.startedAt : 0;
+{ from: current.state.kind, to: next.kind, durationMs, prs: next.prs }
+```
+
+`now` is already declared at the top of `transition()`. Use type-narrowed check on `current.state.kind === 'running'`.
+
+**`src/orchestrator/__tests__/sprint.test.ts`** — add one test after existing `transition` tests using `makeManager` from `./helpers`. Assert `durationMs` is present and matches elapsed wall-clock between start and complete.
+
+## Acceptance criteria
+
+- `durationMs` is in the `sprint.completed` payload when transitioning from `running`
+- `durationMs === 0` if no `running` start time
+- Typecheck + lint + test pass
diff --git a/docs/briefs/feat-wire-classifier-into-smoke.md b/docs/briefs/feat-wire-classifier-into-smoke.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..047d1be
--- /dev/null
+++ b/docs/briefs/feat-wire-classifier-into-smoke.md
@@ -0,0 +1,34 @@
+---
+id: feat-wire-classifier-into-smoke
+title: "feat(scripts): wire classifier into run-smoke.ts routing"
+mode: default
+tags: [feature, scripts]
+source: 22
+---
+
+## Context
+
+Depends on: classifier module from `feat-classifier-brief-to-routing`.
+
+`run-smoke.ts` hardcodes routing:
+
+```ts
+const claudeSpec: WorkerSpec = { provider: 'claude', model: 'claude-sonnet-4-6', workerId: 'claude-max-1' };
+const routing: RoutingDecision = { architect: claudeSpec, editor: claudeSpec, reviewer: reviewerSpec, verify: rawTask.routingHints.verify };
+```
+
+## Goal
+
+Replace hardcoded routing with a call to `classifyTask`:
+
+```ts
+import { classifyTask } from '../src/classifier/index.ts';
+const routing = classifyTask({ title: rawTask.title, body: rawTask.body, labels: rawTask.labels });
+```
+
+## Acceptance criteria
+
+- Routing is driven by `classifyTask` output
+- No hardcoded `WorkerSpec` in the routing block
+- Smoke run completes green
+- Typecheck + lint + test pass
diff --git a/docs/briefs/fix-derive-branch-name.md b/docs/briefs/fix-derive-branch-name.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..bda4cec
--- /dev/null
+++ b/docs/briefs/fix-derive-branch-name.md
@@ -0,0 +1,35 @@
+---
+id: fix-derive-branch-name
+title: "fix(scripts): derive branch name from issue title in run-smoke.ts"
+mode: default
+tags: [bugfix, scripts]
+source: 16
+---
+
+## Problem
+
+The branch name in `run-smoke.ts` is hardcoded:
+
+```ts
+const branchName = `chore/smoke-${rawTask.issueNumber}-remove-stale-todo`;
+```
+
+This only works for the original issue. Any new issue gets a branch named after the stale TODO task.
+
+## Solution
+
+Derive the branch name from `rawTask.title` at runtime:
+
+- Lowercase the title
+- Replace spaces and special chars with `-`
+- Collapse repeated dashes
+- Truncate to ~50 chars
+- Prefix with the conventional-commit prefix from the title (`feat/`, `fix/`, `chore/`) and the issue number
+
+Example: issue #7 "feat: add classifier module" → `feat/smoke-7-add-classifier-module`.
+
+## Acceptance criteria
+
+- Branch name reflects the picked issue's title
+- No hardcoded string references
+- Typecheck + lint + test pass
diff --git a/docs/briefs/fix-empty-queue-error-message.md b/docs/briefs/fix-empty-queue-error-message.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..bc947b9
--- /dev/null
+++ b/docs/briefs/fix-empty-queue-error-message.md
@@ -0,0 +1,30 @@
+---
+id: fix-empty-queue-error-message
+title: "fix(scripts): replace hardcoded issue #6 reference in error message"
+mode: default
+tags: [bugfix, scripts, docs]
+source: 17
+---
+
+## Problem
+
+When the queue is empty, `run-smoke.ts` prints:
+
+```
+ERROR: No issues ready to pick. Ensure issue #6 has label auto:ship and is open.
+```
+
+This is hardcoded to issue #6 and is confusing once that issue is closed.
+
+## Solution
+
+Replace with a generic message:
+
+```
+ERROR: No issues ready to pick. Create a GitHub issue with the label auto:ship and rerun.
+```
+
+## Acceptance criteria
+
+- Error message contains no hardcoded issue number
+- Typecheck + lint + test pass
diff --git a/docs/briefs/fix-open-pipeline-pr-ready.md b/docs/briefs/fix-open-pipeline-pr-ready.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..bc98aca
--- /dev/null
+++ b/docs/briefs/fix-open-pipeline-pr-ready.md
@@ -0,0 +1,21 @@
+---
+id: fix-open-pipeline-pr-ready
+title: "fix(scripts): open pipeline PRs as ready-to-merge, not draft"
+mode: default
+tags: [bugfix, scripts, dx]
+source: 21
+---
+
+## Problem
+
+`buildPrOpener()` in `scripts/run-smoke.ts` always passes `--draft` to `gh pr create`. Every pipeline PR requires a manual `gh pr ready <N>` before it can be merged, adding friction.
+
+## Solution
+
+Remove the `'--draft'` argument from the `gh pr create` call in `buildPrOpener()`. CI plus reviewer approval already gate the PR. Draft status adds no extra safety and slows down the review-merge loop.
+
+## Acceptance criteria
+
+- Pipeline PRs open in ready state (no draft label on GitHub)
+- Smoke run completes green end-to-end
+- Typecheck + lint + test pass
diff --git a/docs/briefs/fix-reviewer-not-weaker-than-architect.md b/docs/briefs/fix-reviewer-not-weaker-than-architect.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..27c8713
--- /dev/null
+++ b/docs/briefs/fix-reviewer-not-weaker-than-architect.md
@@ -0,0 +1,29 @@
+---
+id: fix-reviewer-not-weaker-than-architect
+title: "fix(classifier): reviewer must not resolve weaker than architect on rule-override paths"
+mode: ralph
+tags: [bugfix, classifier]
+source: 44
+---
+
+## Symptom
+
+For tasks where the scorer would have chosen a low tier (e.g. Haiku) but a routing rule promotes the architect, the reviewer slot keeps mirroring the scorer-derived tier instead of tracking the architect's final model. The reviewer can therefore end up weaker than the architect on the same task.
+
+## Why it matters
+
+The reviewer's job is a second opinion at architect strength. A weaker reviewer can rubber-stamp work that a stronger architect produced, which defeats the cross-check the pipeline relies on.
+
+## Reproduction
+
+1. Open an issue with a title matching an architect routing rule (e.g. mentioning "design", "security review", or "migration").
+2. Do not add `complexity:high`, `priority:high`, or any other tier-bumping label.
+3. Run the classifier on this task.
+4. Observe the architect resolves to Sonnet (rule-promoted) while the reviewer resolves to Haiku.
+
+## Acceptance criteria
+
+- The reviewer's model is always at the same tier or higher than the architect's model in the same classification result.
+- Invariant holds across every path: scorer-only, rule-override, complexity label, manual override.
+- New regression tests cover each of the four paths.
+- Typecheck + lint + test pass.
diff --git a/docs/briefs/fix-sentry-undefined-flush.md b/docs/briefs/fix-sentry-undefined-flush.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..234c2d7
--- /dev/null
+++ b/docs/briefs/fix-sentry-undefined-flush.md
@@ -0,0 +1,33 @@
+---
+id: fix-sentry-undefined-flush
+title: "fix(pipeline): guard runPipeline against undefined Sentry client on flush"
+mode: ralph
+tags: [bugfix, observability, sentry]
+source: 80
+---
+
+## Symptom
+
+Sentry issue: `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'flush')` thrown from `src/pipeline/runner.ts` inside `runPipeline`.
+
+## Hypothesis
+
+The pipeline runner calls `sentry.flush()` at end-of-run. When Sentry is disabled in dev or when the init step bailed out (missing DSN), the module-level `sentry` reference is `undefined`. The call site does not guard the access.
+
+## Repro
+
+1. Unset `SENTRY_DSN`.
+2. Trigger a pipeline run via `scripts/run-smoke.ts`.
+3. Observe the crash at runtime when the runner reaches the cleanup hook.
+
+## Acceptance criteria
+
+- `runPipeline` cleanup uses optional chaining or an explicit guard before calling `flush`.
+- A unit test covers the runner exiting cleanly when no Sentry client is configured.
+- Existing Sentry-enabled path still flushes events on success and failure.
+- Typecheck + lint + test pass.
+
+## Out of scope
+
+- Re-architecting Sentry initialisation.
+- Adding new Sentry tags or spans.
diff --git a/docs/briefs/test-complexity-low-defeats-rule-promotion.md b/docs/briefs/test-complexity-low-defeats-rule-promotion.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..ad1ad71
--- /dev/null
+++ b/docs/briefs/test-complexity-low-defeats-rule-promotion.md
@@ -0,0 +1,28 @@
+---
+id: test-complexity-low-defeats-rule-promotion
+title: "test(classifier): complexity:low must defeat a rule-driven opus promotion"
+mode: tdd
+tags: [test, classifier]
+source: 43
+---
+
+## Context
+
+PR #41 introduced the policy that the architect role runs on Sonnet by default and is only promoted to Opus when the issue carries a `complexity:high` label. `complexity:low` is also supposed to force Sonnet even when something else (scorer or routing rule) would otherwise push to Opus.
+
+The PR ships an explicit test for `complexity:low` defeating a **scorer-driven** Opus promotion. It does not test the same outcome for a **rule-driven** Opus promotion (the routing rules that hardcode Opus for architect, like the SQL/migrations rule). The behaviour is correct by code trace but not exercised by the test suite.
+
+## Acceptance criteria
+
+- A new unit test exists that:
+  - Submits a task whose title or body matches a routing rule mapping `architect → claude-opus-4-7`
+  - Includes `complexity:low` in the labels
+  - Asserts the resulting architect model resolves to `claude-sonnet-4-6`
+- A second unit test asserts the same outcome when no complexity label is present at all (the default cap), distinct from the `complexity:low` case
+- All existing tests still pass
+
+## Out of scope
+
+- Changing the routing rule shape or schema
+- Refactoring the classifier
+- Adding new label types

title: feat(pipeline): doctor extension — fingerprints, Haiku scan, daily rollup
body:
## Behavior

The doctor gains three new cadences beyond its current on-demand CI-fix role:

1. **Real-time fingerprint match** — before any LLM call, the CI log is matched against a table of known failure signatures. A match immediately applies a recovery strategy (escalate model, retry clean, double timeout) without spending tokens.

2. **Periodic Haiku scan (15 min)** — a background `DoctorScheduler` reads recent failure events and asks Haiku to identify emerging patterns. Uses its own dedicated single-worker Haiku pool so scans cannot starve active tasks.

3. **Daily rollup (midnight)** — aggregates the day's failure patterns and appends a dated section to `<repo>/.ifleet/learnings.md` so the auto-router (#75) can load them on the next sprint.

Five new recovery strategies:
- Architect fails twice → escalate to opus
- Reviewer rejects twice for the same reason → bump model + flip provider
- Verify timeout → double the timeout and retry once
- Worktree allocation conflict → prune and retry
- Doctor cap hit → post Discord message (✅ = skip, ❌ = fail, 30-min silence = fail)

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] A CI log containing `"Cannot find module"` or `"TS2307"` matches the `tsc-no-module` fingerprint
- [ ] Fingerprint match is applied before any Haiku/runDoctor call (verified via attempt count in tests)
- [ ] Two architect failures escalate the architect spec to opus and retry; already-at-opus → fail with `'architect failed at max tier'`
- [ ] Reviewer concerns repeated across two rounds (first 40 chars overlap) trigger model escalation + provider flip
- [ ] A verify timeout doubles once; a second timeout fails the task normally
- [ ] Doctor cap hit **with** `DISCORD_IFLEET_WEBHOOK` configured: posts Discord message with ✅ / ❌ instructions, polls for reaction, 30-min timeout auto-fails
- [ ] Doctor cap hit **without** Discord URL: preserves current fail-immediately behavior
- [ ] `DoctorScheduler.runScan()` fires every 15 min, emits `doctor.scan_report` event, writes `.omc/sprints/doctor-scan-latest.json`
- [ ] Daily rollup fires at midnight (wall clock), appends `## YYYY-MM-DD` section to `.ifleet/learnings.md` without overwriting existing content
- [ ] Rollup creates `.ifleet/learnings.md` if it does not exist
- [ ] All strategies have per-task retry caps — no infinite loops possible

## Implementation notes

**New files:**

| File | Purpose | ~Lines |
|---|---|---|
| `src/pipeline/fingerprints.ts` | Schema, 5 built-in fingerprints, `matchFingerprint()` | +80 |
| `src/pipeline/recovery-strategies.ts` | Strategies 1/2/3/5/7 implementations | +120 |
| `src/pipeline/doctor-scheduler.ts` | `DoctorScheduler` class — scan + rollup | +150 |
| `src/pipeline/__tests__/fingerprints.test.ts` | Fingerprint match tests | +40 |
| `src/pipeline/__tests__/recovery.test.ts` | Strategy unit tests | +80 |
| `src/pipeline/__tests__/doctor-scheduler.test.ts` | Scan + rollup tests (mock workerPool) | +60 |

**Modified files:**

| File | Change | ~Lines |
|---|---|---|
| `src/pipeline/runner.ts` | Fingerprint check before doctor, strategy hooks, cap-hit handler | +60 |
| `src/pipeline/types.ts` | Add `verifyTimeoutMs?`, `verifyDoubled?` to `PipelineInput` | +5 |
| `src/orchestrator/index.ts` | Instantiate `DoctorScheduler`, pass `eventLog`, wire Discord URL and dedicated scanner pool | +30 |

Total: ~625 lines net new.

**Built-in fingerprints (v1 seed):**

| id | patterns | strategy |
|---|---|---|
| `tsc-no-module` | `"Cannot find module"`, `"TS2307"` | `escalate_model` |
| `esm-require` | `"ERR_REQUIRE_ESM"`, `"Cannot use import statement"` | `escalate_model` |
| `lint-fail` | `"ESLint"`, `"lint failed"` | `escalate_model` |
| `test-timeout` | `"Timeout"`, `"exceeded"`, `"jest --testTimeout"` | `double_timeout` |
| `worktree-conflict` | `"worktree already exists"`, `"already locked"` | `retry_clean` |

**Scanner pool (Q5):** Dedicated single-worker Haiku pool wired via `OrchestratorOptions.scannerWorkerPool?: WorkerPool`. Omitting it disables the scheduler cleanly.

**Rollup timing (Q4):** `DoctorScheduler` calculates ms until next 00:00 local time on start, schedules first rollup with `setTimeout`, repeats with `setInterval` every 24 h.

**Doctor cap Discord gate (Q3):** Polls Discord message for `✅`/`+1`/`rocket`/`hooray` (skip) or `❌` (fail). 30-min no-reaction → auto-fail.

## Out of scope

- Worktree prune implementation (belongs in `PipelineRunnerFactory`, separate issue)
- Extending fingerprint table beyond v1 seed set
- Strategy 2 `allowProviderFlip` field (can be revisited if cross-provider rule conflicts)

## Dependencies

- Soft dependency on #69 (3a — learnings.md path) for rollup destination
- Soft dependency on #75 (4a — auto-router) to consume what rollup writes; can land independently

title: feat(classifier): sprint mode routing + Haiku auto-router
body:
## Behavior

When a sprint is submitted, before any tasks are dispatched, a lightweight Haiku call analyses the sprint goal against accumulated learnings and returns a routing decision. The decision sets the sprint mode, surfaces risk flags visible in observability, and can pause the sprint to request human clarification (deep-interview).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] A sprint in `queued` state transitions to `planning` before any tasks are assigned
- [ ] A Haiku call is made during `planning` with the sprint goal and learnings content
- [ ] The `RouterDecision` is stored on `SprintRecord.routerDecision` and emitted as `sprint.router_decision`
- [ ] Any Haiku failure (timeout, malformed output, missing pool) results in safe fallback and sprint continues normally
- [ ] When `deepInterviewRequired: true`, sprint transitions to `paused` with reason `'deep-interview required'`, IFleet posts a Discord message with the Haiku-generated questions, then polls that Discord message for a ✅ reaction; on reaction → `Orchestrator.resumeSprint()`
- [ ] When `deepInterviewRequired: false`, sprint transitions to `running` after router completes
- [ ] If `.ifleet/learnings.md` is missing, router runs with empty learnings (no error)
- [ ] Existing tests pass without wiring `workerPool` (backward-compatible optional field)

## Implementation notes

**Insertion point:** `SprintManager.tick()` in `src/orchestrator/sprint.ts`

```typescript
if (sprint.state.kind === 'queued') {
  this.transition(sprintId, { kind: 'planning' });
  const decision = await this.runAutoRouter(sprint);
  this.applyRouterDecision(sprintId, decision);
  return;
}
if (sprint.state.kind === 'planning') {
  return; // router in flight
}
```

**New file:** `src/orchestrator/auto-router.ts` (~60 lines) — prompt builder, Haiku call, parser, safe fallback.

**Safe fallback (any error path):**
```typescript
{ recommendedMode: 'normal', deepInterviewRequired: false, deepInterviewQuestions: [], riskFlags: [], learningsApplied: false, raw: '' }
```

**Deep-interview resume (Q1 decision):** `onDeepInterviewRequired` callback posts via `DISCORD_IFLEET_WEBHOOK` with the Haiku-generated questions, then polls the Discord message for a qualifying reaction (`✅`, `+1`, `rocket`, `hooray`). On detection → `Orchestrator.resumeSprint()`. A dedicated `DiscordReactionPoller` handles the poll loop at 30-s interval with no hard timeout (waits until Seb reacts).

**File-level changes:**

| File | Change | ~Lines |
|---|---|---|
| `src/orchestrator/types.ts` | Add `RouterDecision`, extend `SprintRecord` | +20 |
| `src/orchestrator/sprint.ts` | Options, `tick()` planning gate, `runAutoRouter()`, `applyRouterDecision()` | +80 |
| `src/orchestrator/auto-router.ts` | **New file** | +60 |
| `src/orchestrator/index.ts` | Wire `workerPool`, `repoRoot`, `onDeepInterviewRequired` | +15 |
| `src/orchestrator/__tests__/sprint.test.ts` | Planning gate, fallback, deep-interview pause tests | +60 |

Total: ~235 lines net new.

## Out of scope

- Passing risk flags as synthetic labels into `classifyTask` (Phase 5)
- Overnight mode behavioral changes (Phase 3d)

## Dependencies

- Soft dependency on issue #69 (learnings.md path) — if not landed, router runs with empty learnings, no error

title: feat(classifier): sprint mode routing + Haiku auto-router
body:
## Problem

All sprints use the same system prompt and retry policy regardless of task type. A persistence task needs different instructions than a test-driven task. There is no way to signal intent without writing it into the brief body.

## Solution

Extend the routing system to support sprint modes via GitHub issue labels. Four modes, each mapped to a distinct label:

| Label | Mode | Behavior |
|-------|------|----------|
| `mode:ralph` | Persistence | Editor prompt adds: "Do not stop until the task is fully complete. If CI fails, fix it. If the reviewer rejects, address every concern. Max 5 retry rounds." |
| `mode:ulw` | Speed | Architect plans in bullet points only (no prose). Editor makes one focused change per commit. |
| `mode:tdd` | Test-first | Architect plan must list failing tests to write FIRST, then implementation. Editor writes tests before touching implementation files. |
| `mode:deslop` | Cleanup | Editor prompt adds: "Remove all unnecessary complexity, dead code, unused imports. No new features. Leave the code cleaner than you found it." |

If no `mode:*` label is present, behavior is unchanged (default mode — current behavior).

## Implementation

### Label parsing
Extend `parseLabels` (or equivalent) to extract the mode from the issue's label set and return one of: `ralph | ulw | tdd | deslop | default`.

### Prompt injection
Each mode only changes the system prompt injected into the architect and/or editor — no new pipeline stages, no new agents.

### Threading
Mode must flow: `parseLabels` → routing decision → pipeline input → prompt construction. It must not be inferred from the brief body.

## Acceptance Criteria

Each criterion must be independently testable:

1. **Label → mode mapping** — `parseLabels` returns `ralph` for `mode:ralph`, `ulw` for `mode:ulw`, `tdd` for `mode:tdd`, `deslop` for `mode:deslop`
2. **Distinct prompts** — each of the 4 modes produces a different effective system prompt string compared to default and to each other
3. **Default mode** — absence of any `mode:*` label results in `default` mode with no system prompt change
4. **Thread integrity** — the mode value is correctly passed from label parsing through to the final prompt construction step

## Out of Scope

- Combining multiple modes on a single sprint
- Dynamic mode switching mid-sprint
- New pipeline stages or agents for any mode
- A `mode:` label for the default/no-op case

title: feat(pipeline): per-repo learnings + deep-interview phase in architect
body:
## Problem

Vague briefs cause the architect to make wrong plans. When a brief says "improve the dashboard" or "clean up the auth flow", the architect guesses intent and burns a sprint on the wrong thing. There is no mechanism to ask Seb for clarification before execution begins.

## Solution

Add an interview step BEFORE architect planning. When the brief is short or vague, spawn a haiku call to generate 3 clarifying questions, post them to Discord via `DISCORD_IFLEET_WEBHOOK`, and wait up to 10 minutes for a reply. If no reply arrives, proceed with planning anyway (non-blocking).

## Trigger Conditions

Trigger the interview if EITHER condition is true:
- Task body is shorter than 100 characters
- Task body contains any of these keywords: `improve`, `refactor`, `clean up`, `make better`

Clear briefs (> 100 chars AND no vague keywords) skip the interview entirely.

## Implementation

### Discord posting
- Generate 3 clarifying questions via a haiku call with the brief as input
- Post as a single Discord message with a numbered list (1. ... 2. ... 3. ...)
- Use `DISCORD_IFLEET_WEBHOOK` env variable for the webhook URL
- If `DISCORD_IFLEET_WEBHOOK` is not set, skip the interview silently and log one line

### Waiting
- Use the same polling pattern as `waitForApproval` in `src/queue/issue-commenter.ts`
- Wait up to 10 minutes for a reply reaction or message on the Discord thread
- After 10 minutes, log "interview timeout — proceeding with planning" and continue

### Non-blocking guarantee
A timeout or Discord error must never prevent the sprint from proceeding. All interview failures degrade gracefully to a skip.

## Acceptance Criteria

Each criterion must be independently testable:

1. **Short brief triggers interview** — a task body with fewer than 100 chars causes the interview step to run
2. **Vague keyword triggers interview** — a task body containing "refactor" (or any listed keyword) causes the interview step to run
3. **Clear brief skips interview** — a task body with > 100 chars and no vague keywords skips the interview entirely
4. **Missing webhook skips interview** — when `DISCORD_IFLEET_WEBHOOK` is not set, the interview is skipped and a log line is emitted
5. **Timeout does not block** — when the 10-minute window elapses with no reply, the sprint continues to architect planning

## Out of Scope

- Parsing or interpreting the reply content
- Multi-turn conversation (ask → answer → follow-up)
- Changing the architect system prompt based on answers (Phase 4)
- Any UI for managing interview responses

title: feat(pipeline): per-repo learnings + deep-interview phase in architect
body:
## Problem

IFleet has no memory across sprints. Each sprint starts cold. Repeated mistakes are never learned — the architect makes the same wrong assumptions sprint after sprint with no way to improve.

## Solution

A lightweight learnings file at `<worktree>/.ifleet/learnings.md` (created if missing). The architect reads it before planning and the failure handler appends a short lesson after each sprint failure.

## Implementation

### Architect phase change
Before generating the plan, read `.ifleet/learnings.md` from the worktree root. If the file exists and is non-empty, append a section to the architect system prompt:

```
## Repo learnings
{contents of .ifleet/learnings.md}
```

If the file is missing or empty, no change to the prompt.

### Failure handler change
On `task.failed` event: append a new line to `.ifleet/learnings.md` in the worktree:

```
- [ISO date] {failureReason}
```

If the file does not exist, create it with that line as the first entry. After writing, commit and push the file from the worktree.

## Acceptance Criteria

Each criterion must be independently testable:

1. **Learnings injected** — when `.ifleet/learnings.md` is non-empty, the architect system prompt includes the `## Repo learnings` section with the file contents
2. **Learnings omitted** — when `.ifleet/learnings.md` is missing or empty, the architect system prompt has no learnings section
3. **Failure appends** — when a sprint fails, a new lesson line is appended to `.ifleet/learnings.md`
4. **File created on first failure** — if `.ifleet/learnings.md` does not exist, the failure handler creates it with the lesson as the first line

## Out of Scope

- RAG or vector search over learnings
- Cross-repo sharing of learnings
- UI for viewing or editing learnings
- Summarizing or compressing old learnings

title: feat(classifier): reviewer >= architect invariant + complexity:low rule-cap test
body:
## Symptom
For tasks where the scorer would have chosen a low tier (e.g. haiku) but a routing rule promotes the architect, the reviewer slot keeps mirroring the scorer-derived tier instead of tracking the architect's final model. The reviewer can therefore end up weaker than the architect on the same task.

## Why it matters
The reviewer's job is a second opinion at architect strength. A weaker reviewer can rubber-stamp work that a stronger architect produced, which defeats the cross-check the pipeline relies on.

## Reproduction
1. Open an issue with a title that matches one of the architect routing rules (e.g. text mentioning "design", "security review", or "migration")
2. Do not add `complexity:high`, `priority:high`, or any other tier-bumping label
3. Run the classifier on this task
4. Observe the architect resolves to Sonnet (rule promoted, then the opus cap applied) while the reviewer resolves to Haiku

## Acceptance criteria
- The reviewer's model is always at the same tier or higher than the architect's model in the same classification result
- This invariant holds across every path:
  - scorer-only (no rule match, no complexity label)
  - rule-override (architect role rule matches, with and without complexity labels)
  - explicit `complexity:high`
  - explicit `complexity:low`
- A unit test enforces the invariant for each of those four paths

## Out of scope
- Changing tier definitions
- Introducing new label types
- Touching the architect opus cap from PR #41

## History
The behavior pre-dates PR #41 but PR #41's opus cap makes the gap easier to surface, so the cleanup belongs in the same family of work. Flagged by the independent reviewer of PR #41.

**AFK** — agent can execute autonomously.

title: feat(classifier): reviewer >= architect invariant + complexity:low rule-cap test
body:
## Context
PR #41 introduced the policy that the architect role runs on Sonnet by default and is only promoted to Opus when the issue carries a `complexity:high` label. `complexity:low` is also supposed to force Sonnet even when something else (scorer or routing rule) would otherwise push to Opus.

The PR ships an explicit test for `complexity:low` defeating a **scorer-driven** opus promotion. It does not test the same outcome for a **rule-driven** opus promotion (the routing rules that hardcode opus for architect, like the SQL/migrations rule). The behavior is correct by code trace but not exercised by the test suite.

## Acceptance criteria
- A new unit test exists that:
  - Submits a task whose title or body matches a routing rule that maps `architect → claude-opus-4-7`
  - Includes `complexity:low` in the labels
  - Asserts the resulting architect model resolves to `claude-sonnet-4-6`
- A second unit test asserts the same outcome when no complexity label is present at all (the default cap), distinct from the `complexity:low` case
- All existing tests still pass

## Out of scope
- Changing the routing rule shape or schema
- Refactoring the classifier
- Adding new label types

## Notes
Independent reviewer of PR #41 flagged this as the single test gap worth a follow-up. Not a blocker for the merge.

**AFK** — agent can execute autonomously.

title: feat(orchestrator): include durationMs in sprint.completed event payload
feat(classifier): build brief → routing decision module
body:
## Context

\`config/routing.json\` defines keyword and file-glob rules for picking provider/model per task. Nothing reads it yet — routing is hardcoded in \`scripts/run-smoke.ts\`.

## Goal

Build \`src/classifier/index.ts\` that exports:

\`\`\`ts
export function classifyTask(task: { title: string; body: string; labels: string[] }): RoutingDecision
\`\`\`

- Load \`config/routing.json\` at startup
- Match task title + body against \`rules[].match.keywords\` (case-insensitive substring)
- Fall through to \`pipeline\` defaults when no rule matches
- Return a \`RoutingDecision\` shaped like the existing type in \`src/pipeline/types.ts\`

## Acceptance criteria

- \`classifyTask\` returns correct provider/model for keyword matches
- Falls back to \`pipeline.architect\` / \`pipeline.editor\` defaults when no rule matches
- Unit tests cover: keyword hit, no match (default), multi-rule priority (first match wins)
- Typecheck + lint + test pass

title: fix(scripts): derive branch name from issue title in run-smoke.ts
body:
## Problem

The branch name in `run-smoke.ts` is hardcoded:
```ts
const branchName = `chore/smoke-${rawTask.issueNumber}-remove-stale-todo`;
```

This only works for issue #6. Any new issue will get a branch named after the stale TODO task.

## Solution

Derive the branch name from `rawTask.title` at runtime:
- Lowercase the title
- Replace spaces and special chars with `-`
- Truncate to ~50 chars
- Prefix with `chore/smoke-<issueNumber>-`

Example: issue #7 "feat: add classifier module" → `feat/smoke-7-add-classifier-module`

## Acceptance criteria

- Branch name reflects the picked issue's title
- No hardcoded string references
- Typecheck + lint + test pass
